text.title: QA Performance Run Verdict Template Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the qa-perf-run-verdict-template methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Verdict is exactly one of PASS, FAIL, INCONCLUSIVE. No 'mostly ok'.
rule.rationale: Binary verdicts cut decision latency; soft verdicts let releases ship while regressing.

rule.statement: Verdict is computed against SLOs declared in ops/slo.yaml before the run. SLOs invented after the run are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc SLOs justify the result; pre-declared SLOs falsify it.

rule.statement: Verdict requires ≥1000 samples per endpoint (or N agreed in slo.yaml). Below floor the verdict is INCONCLUSIVE.

rationale.source: Statistics — Central Limit Theorem in practice.

rule.rationale: Small samples produce noisy percentiles; conclusions drawn from <1k samples are unsafe.

rule.statement: Verdict records environment fingerprint (host, cpu, dataset id, traffic-shape id).
rule.rationale: Without fingerprint, baselines drift silently across infrastructure changes.

rule.statement: Verdict cites the exact baseline run id used; baselines older than 30 days require explicit acknowledgement.
rule.rationale: Stale baselines validate regressions as improvements.

rule.statement: p95 regression >10% vs baseline auto-FAILs even if SLOs are still met (alerts on creeping drift).
rule.rationale: Strict SLO-only gating misses gradual regressions that consume the error budget.

rule.statement: If no SLOs are defined OR product is pre-MVP OR tool is internal-only with no latency contract, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Without SLO definition the verdict has nothing to anchor on.
